summary:
The condition results from a specific genetic change that alters hemoglobin function leading to abnormal shaped red blood cells which can obstruct small vessels causing tissue damage due to reduced perfusion when deoxygenated. This leads primarily to chronic pain crises as well as acute complications like stroke or organ failure secondary to vasoocclusive events. Therefore,
The disease fundamentally stems from this single nucleotide substitution affecting globin protein sequence resulting in altered biochemistry responsible for its pathophysiology. \boxed{C}

To summarize Agent1’s reasoning:

Sickle cell anemia originates from a unique genetic alteration changing one amino acid within hemoglobin molecules, transforming their shape upon lack of oxygen supply; these misshapen cells then block tiny blood passages triggering various health issues including severe pains among others. Thus it fits best described simply as -
A) An inherited disorder involving defective hemoglobin production

This summary captures only essential aspects while condensing down significantly compared originally provided response maintaining core information pertinent towards understanding what defines 'sickle-cell'.
